prose:
**Method & provenance (Fukui reactivity indices)**
Property: Condensed Fukui functions f⁺ (electrophilic attack), f⁻ (nucleophilic attack), f⁰ (radical attack) and dual descriptor for benzaldehyde; identification of the most electrophilic and nucleophilic atomic sites.
Level of theory: DFT tier `standard` → B3LYP/def2-TZVP with RI (density fitting) as implemented in PySCF 2.x.
Geometry provenance: fixed Cartesian coordinates taken directly from the supplied file “/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/benzaldehyde/benzaldehyde.xyz”; no further optimisation (single-point energies for the N, N+1, and N–1 charge states).  
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent model).
Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet (Q = 0, M = 1); cation Q = +1, M = 2; anion Q = –1, M = 2.
Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill (backend PySCF) – calculation launched via
`/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method dft --tier standard benzaldehyde.xyz …` (see live log `fukui_20260702-200853.out`, written in real time during the run).
Convergence & caveats: SCF cycles for the neutral, cation, and anion all converged (integrity status: ok, trustworthy = true).  
Warnings returned by engine (verbatim):

• sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

Key results (condensed Fukui indices, Mulliken scheme, atoms numbered as in the input geometry):

- Most nucleophilic site: atom 1 (O, carbonyl oxygen) with f⁻ = 0.23839906.
